personal.name: Trupal Patel
personal.title: Full-Stack Software Engineer
personal.storyText:
I am a Full-Stack Software Engineer specializing in building scalable, production-grade SaaS platforms, real-time data systems, and AI-driven applications. With a strong foundation in JavaScript, TypeScript, and modern frontend frameworks like React and Next.js, I focus on delivering high-performance, user-centric solutions backed by robust backend architectures.

My work spans across multi-tenant SaaS ecosystems, computer vision data pipelines, and enterprise analytics platforms. At Allyvia, I played a key role in engineering a multi-tenant retail operations system with deep integrations into financial systems like QuickBooks, while at AIVID.AI, I contributed to large-scale real-time monitoring platforms processing millions of data points across distributed systems.

I combine strong system design thinking with hands-on engineering, enabling me to architect solutions that are both scalable and maintainable. My passion lies in solving complex problems, optimizing performance, and building systems that operate reliably at scale.
personal.voiceSummary: Hi, I'm Trupal Patel. I specialize in building scalable, production-grade Software as a Service platforms, real time systems, and A I driven apps. My work spans from building a multi-tenant retail system for Allyvia with complex QuickBooks integration, to contributing to real-time analytics platforms processing millions of data points at Aivid A I. I combine system design with hands-on engineering to solve complex performance problems. Connect with me on the socials page or download my resume.

achievements: Enabled scalable onboarding of multiple organizations.
Reduced frontend code duplication through modular architecture.
Delivered secure and enterprise-grade access control.
Stabilized third-party integrations for production use.

deepDive.systemOverview: The system was designed as a full-stack SaaS application where each tenant (company) had isolated data, role-based access control, and configurable modules such as inventory management, CRM, employee tracking, billing, and analytics dashboards.
deepDive.responsibilities: I owned the end-to-end development of critical modules across both frontend and backend layers. On the frontend, I utilized React, TypeScript, and Material UI to build scalable and reusable components. I architected a registry-driven navigation system that dynamically rendered UI elements based on user roles and permissions. On the backend side, I worked with secure API design patterns, JWT-based authentication, and middleware-driven authorization to ensure data integrity and security across tenants.
challenges: Implementing multi-tenant data isolation while maintaining performance. Each tenant required strict separation of data without duplicating infrastructure unnecessarily.
Designing a fine-grained RBAC (Role-Based Access Control) system where UI components, routes, and backend APIs all respected permission boundaries consistently.
Integrating QuickBooks APIs, which are complex and stateful, requiring handling asynchronous workflows, webhook reliability, and data synchronization without breaking user experience.
solutions: Designed a permission-aware rendering system, where UI components were dynamically controlled based on decoded JWT permissions. This ensured both security and flexibility.
Introduced a registry-based architecture for navigation and module configuration, significantly reducing code duplication and making it easier to scale new modules.
Implemented robust webhook handlers and retry mechanisms, ensuring reliable financial data synchronization even under failure scenarios.
impact: Enabled scalable onboarding of multiple organizations
Reduced frontend code duplication through modular architecture
Delivered secure and enterprise-grade access control
Stabilized third-party integrations for production use

achievements: Enabled real-time monitoring across 200+ distinct sites.
Enhanced system scalability and background job reliability.

deepDive.systemOverview: The system relied heavily on Elasticsearch for analytics, Node.js for backend processing, and React with Elastic UI for real-time dashboards. Data pipelines handled continuous ingestion from computer vision systems and transformed it into actionable insights.
deepDive.responsibilities: I focused on optimizing Elasticsearch queries and building real-time dashboards that displayed large volumes of analytical data without performance degradation. I also developed automated reporting systems that triggered alerts across multiple communication channels such as email, WhatsApp, Slack, and Twilio. Additionally, I contributed to enabling RTSP-based live streaming, allowing users to visualize real-time processed video feeds.
challenges: Handling massive-scale data aggregation in Elasticsearch, especially when queries involved complex aggregations across millions of records.
Ensuring real-time responsiveness of dashboards without blocking the UI thread or overwhelming the browser.
solutions: Optimized Elasticsearch queries by restructuring aggregations and indexing strategies, achieving a ~25% performance improvement.
Implemented memoization and efficient state management techniques on the frontend to ensure smooth rendering of large datasets.
Designed cron-based distributed job handlers that executed dynamic workflows and triggered webhooks reliably.

achievements: Improved UI responsiveness rendering massive JSON structures.
Strengthened internal Elasticsearch and Kibana analytics tooling.

deepDive.systemOverview: A framework visualizing computer vision intelligence network streams mapping AI inference metrics at scale.
deepDive.responsibilities: I developed over 35+ dashboards using React, TypeScript, and ApexCharts, focusing on visualizing complex AI-generated datasets. I also contributed to Elasticsearch and Kibana plugin development, gaining deep exposure to internal system pipelines.
challenges: Rendering large JSON datasets efficiently in the browser without performance bottlenecks was a major hurdle.
Understanding and adapting to Elastic UI and Kibana’s complex architecture required rapid learning and experimentation.
solutions: Built modular and reusable chart components with optimized data transformations, ensuring smooth rendering even with large payloads.

achievements: Built strong foundational knowledge in the React ecosystem.
Gained hands-on experience with external live API integrations.
Constructed rigid state management pipelines using Redux hooks.

deepDive.systemOverview: Consumer-facing responsive web applications targeting real-time public data distribution.
deepDive.responsibilities: I developed a news web application and a COVID-19 live dashboard, both consuming real-time APIs and updating the UI dynamically heavily focused on React and Redux.
challenges: Managing asynchronous data flows cleanly on component mount boundaries.
Maintaining global state consistency reliably as early developmental challenges.
solutions: Implemented structured Redux patterns and separated concerns between UI rendering limits and active data logic using React hooks.
impact: Built strong foundational knowledge in React ecosystem
Gained hands-on experience with API integration and state management
